heading: Public recreation use of fish and wildlife conservation areas; compatibility with conservation purposes; appropriate incidental or secondary use; consistency with other Federal operations and primary objectives of particular areas; curtailment; forms of recreation not directly related to primary purposes of individual areas; repeal or amendment of provisions for particular areas
label: 460k Public recreation use of fish and wildlife conservation areas; compatibility with conservation purposes; appropriate incidental or secondary use; consistency with other Federal operations and primary objectives of particular areas; curtailment; forms of recreation not directly related to primary purposes of individual areas; repeal or amendment of provisions for particular areas
notes: Amendments1966— substituted “areas within the National Wildlife Refuge System” for “national wildlife refuges, game ranges” in introductory text.Pub. L. 89–669
Short Title, which enacted this subchapter, is popularly known as the “Refuge Recreation Act”.Pub. L. 87–714
amendments: Amendments1966— substituted “areas within the National Wildlife Refuge System” for “national wildlife refuges, game ranges” in introductory text.Pub. L. 89–669

children.label: a
children.text: that such recreational use will not interfere with the primary purposes for which the areas were established, and

children.label: b
children.text: that funds are available for the development, operation, and maintenance of these permitted forms of recreation. This section shall not be construed to repeal or amend previous enactments relating to particular areas.
